name: Chiang Mai University main campus
notes: Major west-side demand anchor spanning education, hospital, staff, student, and visitor trips near the outer ring.

name: Maharaj / Suan Dok medical cluster
notes: Regional hospital cluster feeding west-side and city-center demand beyond the old city edge.

name: MAYA Lifestyle Shopping Center
notes: Key west/northwest lifestyle anchor linking Nimman, CMU, and the 121 access side.

name: One Nimman
notes: Strong lifestyle and tourism destination reinforcing west-side urban charging demand.

name: Nimman commercial spine
notes: Dense mixed-use commercial strip capturing daily retail, dining, office, and visitor demand in the Nimman core.

name: Chiang Mai Zoo
notes: Major west-side family and tourism anchor directly relevant to the CMU / Huay Kaew / 121 side.

name: Chiang Mai International Convention and Exhibition Centre
notes: Large exhibition and event venue strengthening the northwest outer-ring catchment beyond Nimman.

name: 700th Anniversary Chiang Mai Sports Complex
notes: Large north-west sports and event anchor near the outer ring and Mae Rim approach.

name: Ton Payom Market
notes: Dense local market and food anchor reinforcing the west-city edge between CMU and canal-road traffic.

name: Kad Na Mor Market
notes: Student and neighborhood market anchor between CMU and Nimman with steady day-evening traffic.

name: Chiang Mai Ram Hospital
notes: Major private hospital close to the old city and the west-inner-city medical catchment.

name: Chiang Mai Gate Market
notes: Important south-old-city market anchor linking local commerce, commuting, and evening food traffic.

name: Warorot Market
notes: Historic city-market anchor with daily retail, wholesale, and visitor traffic on the Ping-side urban core.

name: Chiang Mai Night Bazaar
notes: Strong evening commerce and tourism anchor on the Chang Khlan side of the inner city.

name: Anusarn Market
notes: Night-market and tourism anchor reinforcing the Chang Khlan / Night Bazaar cluster.

name: Tha Phae Gate
notes: Old-city gateway and tourism anchor that strengthens core urban destination demand.

name: Central Chiang Mai Airport
notes: Top airport-side mall anchor and one of the strongest urban charging demand nodes in Chiang Mai.

name: Chiang Mai International Airport
notes: Primary air-travel anchor driving rental-car, tourist, business, and ride-hailing charging demand.

name: Chiang Mai Airport arrivals pickup zone
notes: Dedicated arrival-side pickup and ride-hailing anchor reflecting strong tourist and local receiver traffic at the terminal frontage.

name: Chiang Mai Airport rental-car cluster
notes: Airport-side rental-car handover and return cluster supporting high EV turnover potential from fly-drive tourism and business trips.

name: Mahidol airport frontage corridor
notes: High-friction frontage corridor between the terminal approach and Central Airport with dense pickup, drop-off, and through-traffic demand.

name: Chiang Mai Airport operations and support cluster
notes: Airport-side staff, logistics, and support-services anchor reflecting recurring worker and service-vehicle trips beyond passenger flows.

name: Central Chiangmai Memorial Hospital
notes: Important city-south hospital anchor on the Mahidol-Lamphun side.

name: Rajavej Chiang Mai Hospital
notes: Major private hospital on the Mahidol / Chiang Mai-Lamphun corridor.

name: Bangkok Hospital Chiang Mai
notes: Large private hospital reinforcing the east-inner-city and Chiang Mai-Lamphun corridor demand.

name: Chiang Mai Business Park
notes: Business and office cluster on the east-inner-city side near Superhighway and Arcade catchments.

name: Chiang Mai Bus Terminal 3 / Arcade
notes: Primary regional bus terminal for the city and a strong dwell-time transport anchor.

name: Central Festival Chiangmai
notes: Major north-east city mall and one of the strongest modern retail anchors in urban Chiang Mai.

name: Big C Extra Chiang Mai
notes: Dense north-east retail anchor already hosting EV competition and relevant cross-shopping demand.

name: Lanna Hospital
notes: Important north-city hospital on the Kham Tiang side serving urban daily trips.

name: Nakornping Hospital
notes: Key public hospital on the north-west city side serving Chang Phueak, Mae Rim approach, and ring-road-linked trips.

name: Lotus's Kham Tiang
notes: North-city retail anchor on the Superhighway and Kham Tiang corridor.

name: Makro Chiang Mai
notes: Bulk-retail destination strengthening the north-city and Superhighway catchment.

name: McCormick Hospital
notes: Significant east-inner-city hospital reinforcing the business park and Arcade side of the city.

name: Big C Mae Hia
notes: Important southwest retail anchor between airport, Mae Hia, and Hang Dong access roads.

name: Mae Hia Fresh Market
notes: Southwest local retail and food anchor on the Mae Hia side of the outer ring.

name: Mae Hia corridor center
notes: Community-commercial axis linking canal road, outer ring, and Hang Dong access demand.

name: Lotus's Hang Dong
notes: Major Hang Dong corridor retail anchor near Route 108 and the outer ring fringe.

name: Kad Farang Village
notes: West-southwest lifestyle and supermarket cluster already used in the hotspot model; should also exist as a POI anchor.

name: Chiang Mai Night Safari
notes: Tourism and evening destination anchor on the Mae Hia-Hang Dong side of the outer ring.

name: Royal Park Rajapruek
notes: Large southwest attraction and event destination influencing Mae Hia / canal-road demand.

name: MeeChok Plaza
notes: Key north-side community retail anchor on the Ruamchok-Fa Ham side and an important ring-road demand node.

name: Fa Ham / Ruamchok community axis

name: Theppanya Hospital
notes: Important north-side hospital anchor supporting the Fa Ham-Ruamchok catchment near the outer ring.

name: Star Avenue Arcade
notes: Retail and dining anchor strengthening the north-east urban edge between Superhighway and Fa Ham.

name: The Prince Royal's College
notes: Large school campus and commuter anchor on the Fa Ham-central festival side of the city.

name: Saraphi / Yang Noeng town center
notes: South-east Chiang Mai town center on the Chiang Mai-Lamphun gateway corridor with commuter and local retail demand.

name: Saraphi Hospital
notes: Key hospital anchor on the Chiang Mai-Lamphun corridor serving Saraphi and nearby commuter catchments.

name: Yang Noeng Market
notes: Traditional market and local commercial anchor beside Route 106 corridor traffic.

name: Mahachok Park
notes: East-side roadside/community target anchor on the outer ring and Route 118 approach.

name: MAIIAM Contemporary Art Museum
notes: Strong east-side tourism and destination anchor on the San Kamphaeng corridor.

name: Punn Suk Park
notes: San Kamphaeng corridor community-destination anchor with direct relevance to EVI screening.

name: Doi Saket town center

name: Doi Saket Hospital
notes: Main hospital anchor for Doi Saket-side urban and corridor demand.

name: Tweechol Botanical Garden / resort
notes: East-side leisure and event destination on the Chiang Mai-Doi Saket corridor. Keep it as a destination anchor, but do not let it behave like an urban commercial core for nearby corridor cells.

name: Mae On district center
notes: Eastern district anchor on the corridor toward San Kamphaeng hot springs and Lampang-side travel.

name: San Kamphaeng Hot Springs
notes: Flagship eastern tourism destination feeding through-traffic and dwell-time demand on the Mae On corridor.

name: The Kad Farang Mae Rim
notes: Open-air retail and dining project on Route 107 in Mae Rim with strong destination pull, active parking demand, and recognizable lifestyle positioning in the district.

name: Rimping Supermarket (Kad Farang Mae Rim)
notes: Strong daily-needs retail anchor inside The Kad Farang Mae Rim and one of the clearest repeat-trip magnets for the site.

name: Starbucks The Kad Farang Mae Rim
notes: International coffee anchor that helps validate all-day dwell, meetings, and affluent stopover behavior at the project.

name: Mr. D.I.Y. Mae Rim branch
notes: Value-retail anchor inside Kad Farang Mae Rim supporting routine errands and repeat local trips beyond weekend tourism.

name: Chiang Dao town center
notes: Northbound district center on Route 107 with tourism, market, and intercity stop demand.

name: Chiang Dao Hospital
notes: Key hospital anchor for the Chiang Dao catchment and Route 107 stopover demand.

name: Chiang Dao bus stop / transport cluster
notes: Intercity transport stop on the Chiang Mai-Fang corridor.

name: Chai Prakan town center
notes: North corridor service town between Chiang Dao and Fang with strong roadside and community pull.

name: Chai Prakan Hospital
notes: District hospital anchor for the Chai Prakan section of the Route 107 corridor.

name: Lotus's Go Fresh Chai Prakan
notes: Modern retail anchor reinforcing Chai Prakan's corridor stop role.

name: Phrao town center
notes: Northeast valley district center relevant to the Chiang Rai and Phayao approach corridors.

name: Phrao Hospital
notes: Main healthcare anchor for Phrao and adjacent valley districts.

name: Mae Taeng town center
notes: Northbound service town between the Chiang Mai core and Chiang Dao/Fang corridor.

name: Fang city center
notes: Town-center anchor for Wiang Fang and the main commercial strip on Route 107.

name: Lotus's Fang
notes: Large retail anchor on Chiang Mai-Mae Chan Highway [107] in Wiang, Fang.

name: Makro Fang
notes: Wholesale retail anchor on Route 107 south of central Fang; supports regional shopping trips.

name: Fang Hospital

name: Fang bus station / Yanyon Nakorn Chiang Mai branch
notes: Intercity bus node beside Fang Hospital with frequent services linking Fang, Mae Ai, Thaton, Chai Prakan, Chiang Dao, and Chiang Mai.

name: Fang Walking Street
notes: Sunday market and evening retail/tourism cluster in the town center.

name: Route 107 Fang commercial axis
notes: Main roadside demand spine through Fang town on the Chiang Mai-Mae Chan corridor.

name: Fang Immigration Checkpoint
notes: Government-service anchor in central Fang with weekday visit traffic.

name: Fang District Public Health Office
notes: District public-health office in the same core cluster as the walking street and municipal services.

name: Fang Hot Springs
notes: Flagship tourism destination west of town within Doi Pha Hom Pok National Park.

name: Chiang Mai Provincial Transport Office Fang branch
notes: Vehicle-registration and licensing anchor on Route 107 south of Fang town.

name: Mae Ai Hospital
notes: Important northern catchment hospital serving Mae Ai and border-adjacent communities.

name: Wat Tha Ton
notes: Major Mae Ai / Thaton tourism and pilgrimage anchor linked to the Fang-Thaton travel corridor.
